frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
generated_comment: Aaj metro station pe ek girl ko dekha in a very short dress, and then obviously sab dekh rahe the. Delhi jaisi jagah mein agar aap itna bold kapde pehen ke nikloge toh attention toh milega hi, phir baad mein complain karne ka kya matlab. Simple si baat hai, decent dressing se respect milta hai, public place hai not a fashion show.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it blames women for men's staring and frames respect as conditional on dressing traditionally or 'decently.' It encodes the surface manifestation that non-traditional clothing makes women responsible for harassment and less respectable.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 26. Single. A man in Delhi. You are posting anonymously on a Facebook group called Delhi Confessions after seeing a girl in a short dress at a metro station who was getting stared at by men around her. You are writing your post about what you saw and giving your opinion on why girls who dress a certain way in public spaces are responsible for the attention they get and what they should expect.
